title: QUIC Server-Role Finished Continuation Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the narrow server-role local Finished continuation behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam.
expected_result: The server role emits one local Finished after the supported ClientHello has been accepted, the local ServerHello has been published, local EncryptedExtensions has been published, local Certificate has been published, local CertificateVerify has been published, Handshake keys are available, and the local signing material validates as compatible with the supported single-leaf input; exposes those bytes through the existing Handshake crypto-output seam with correct offset semantics that place the Finished immediately after ServerHello || EncryptedExtensions || Certificate || CertificateVerify; appends the local Finished bytes at the correct transcript boundary immediately after local CertificateVerify; rejects premature, repeated, malformed, incompatible, or unsupported local Finished progression deterministically; and still leaves inbound client Finished proof, server-role commit, and wider handshake support unavailable.
procedure: Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the supported server-role Finished continuation path, outbound local Finished exposure, Handshake key-availability publication ordering, offset semantics, local CertificateVerify gating, transcript append behavior, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection.

Run the prerequisite server-role floor tests that cover the supported ClientHello path, outbound ServerHello exposure, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation, the local Certificate continuation, and the local CertificateVerify continuation.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace markdown views from the updated JSON artifacts, then run `git diff --check` to confirm the slice has no whitespace drift.

Inspect the bridge driver, transcript-progress owner, and key-schedule implementation to confirm the slice stays inside the main library seam, keeps runtime or host wiring unchanged, keeps commit unavailable, and does not add native TLS fallback.
scope: This slice verifies only the permanent server-role continuation from peer ClientHello and local ServerHello through local EncryptedExtensions, local Certificate, local CertificateVerify, and local Finished, plus Handshake packet-protection material availability. It does not prove inbound client Finished proof, server-role commit, client-certificate authentication, trust-store policy, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-26; focused requirement-home tests prove the server-role continuation from the managed CertificateVerify floor into one local Finished flight on the existing handshake seam, including offset semantics, local CertificateVerify gating, deterministic malformed and unsupported prerequisite-state rejection, repeated-progression rejection, and the continued absence of server-role commit. The prerequisite managed CertificateVerify floor remains separately traced by REQ-QUIC-CRT-0115 and its verification evidence.
preconditions: The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.
